data: GCRS noted that Prior arrangement with Pathologist is necessary. Phone No.: 3513 3138. Pathologist contacted.
Make a request by GCRS (via Haematology Laboratory - Hereditary Blood Disease Panel)
Informed patient consent should be obtained and send to the laboratory together with the specimen.

Additional information and guidelines on specimen collection, test requirements, and consultaiton & endorsement are avaiable in the following link: HKCH Genetic and Genomic Pathology Lab Website / Lab Manual

(a) Non-malignant disease (aplastic anaemia included): Peripheral blood AND
  Buccal swabs (preferred) / Hair follicles;

(b) Malignant disease: Buccal swabs / Hair follicles 
    Relatives: Peripheral blood. Post-allo-HSCT subjects: Hair follicles
(1) Peripheral blood: Blood volume required: 2 x 2 mL in K3EDTA Minimal blood volume: 1 x 1 mL in K3EDTA
(2) Buccal swabs: 4 swabs For buccal swab testing (please check all 4 boxes), please use FLOQSwabs®. Please contact GG lab if buccal swabs are required.
(3) Hair follicles: at least 10 pieces with visible follicles.

Additional citrated blood or EDTA blood may be required for phenotypic
  characterisation.

Please handle on case by case manner, as sample types and number
  varied.
Signed consent form is required to send with the specimens.

Contact HKCH for enquiry:
    Clinical consultation:
HKCH Genetic and Genomic Pathology Lab
